expectations.evidence: 5 TypeScript observability scripts generated: observability-logger.ts (9.5KB), metrics-collector.ts (16.9KB), health-check.ts (13.9KB), tracing.ts (11.5KB), monitor.ts (5.8KB), plus a README.md. Exceeds the minimum of 2.

All scripts contain real executable TypeScript classes with substantial logic: ObservabilityLogger has buffered NDJSON output with OTLP HTTP export; MetricsCollector implements counters/gauges/histograms with Prometheus exposition format and HTTP server; HealthChecker provides liveness/readiness/deep probes with actual event-loop-lag measurement, memory-pressure checks, filesystem checks, and API connectivity tests; ObservabilityTracer implements full span lifecycle with OTLP and Chrome Trace Event export; ObservabilityMonitor orchestrates all subsystems. No placeholder-only content.

Scripts and README extensively reference the actual project structure: src/utils/telemetry/instrumentation.ts, src/utils/telemetry/sessionTracing.ts, src/utils/telemetry/perfettoTracing.ts, src/utils/telemetry/bigqueryExporter.ts, src/utils/telemetry/events.ts, src/utils/telemetry/logger.ts, src/services/analytics/sink.ts, src/services/analytics/datadog.ts, src/services/analytics/metadata.ts. README.md has a dedicated 'How Each Script Relates to the Project' section mapping each script to specific source files. Code uses the same environment variables (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, etc.).

None of the 6 output files (5 .ts scripts + README.md) contain any assessment of O1-O4 observability dimensions. The scripts implement observability tooling but do not evaluate or report on the project's standing across the O1 (Feedback), O2 (Persist), O3 (Queryable), O4 (Attribute) framework. There is no mention of 'O1', 'O2', 'O3', 'O4', 'Observability Level', or any dimensional assessment anywhere in the output.
